role: You are a senior full-stack engineer with deep expertise in Vite, React, TypeScript strict mode, IndexedDB, PWA architecture, and YouTube Data API v3.

project.name: OJEET-STUDY
project.type: Public SaaS — Distraction-Free YouTube Study App
project.mission: Enable students to study YouTube content without algorithmic distractions: no recommendations, no comments, no autoplay chaining, no feed exposure. Local-first, offline-capable, installable PWA. Deployed on Vercel.

constraints.non_negotiables: - Framework: Vite + React (latest stable), SPA with React Router v6 - Deployment: Vercel — use vercel.json for SPA fallback routing - UI: Chakra UI — strict white/black minimal theme, no glassmorphism, no gradients, no decorative animations - Icons: lucide-react exclusively — no other icon libraries or emoji - Package manager: PNPM exclusively - Language: TypeScript strict mode throughout - Storage: IndexedDB via Dexie.js + localStorage for config flags only - No Firebase, Supabase, or any external database in MVP - Backend: Vercel Serverless Functions (/api/*) for YouTube API proxying only - Simplicity first — flat component structure, minimal abstraction layers, co-locate logic with its UI when it won't be reused

deliverables.deliverable: Produce a concise, production-grade high-level architecture diagram (as ASCII or structured text). Include: - Data flow: YouTube API → Vercel Serverless Functions → Dexie.js → UI layer - Session tracking pipeline - Service worker layer - PWA install flow - Vercel routing (SPA fallback via vercel.json) Annotate each layer with its responsibility boundary. No generic descriptions — be precise about what each layer owns.

Output a simple, flat folder tree with one-line purpose annotations per file or directory. Avoid deep nesting — prefer co-location over separation. Must include:
/src /pages → Route-level page components (one file per route) /components → Shared, reusable UI primitives only /features → Self-contained feature modules (library, player, analytics, session) — each a single file or small folder /hooks → Custom React hooks shared across features /db → Dexie schema + typed repository functions in one file /services → YouTube API client (calls /api/* routes, never googleapis directly) /utils → Date helpers, CSV serializer, duration formatter /pwa → Service worker registration + install prompt logic
/api → Vercel Serverless Functions (YouTube API proxy) /public → PWA manifest, icons, favicon
Enforce these rules explicitly: - /api/* is the sole layer that touches the YouTube API key - /components never imports from /features - /services calls /api/* routes — never googleapis.com directly - Prefer a single file per feature unless it exceeds ~300 lines

Design a typed Dexie.js schema. Provide the full TypeScript interface definitions and Dexie table declarations for:
Table: videos - id (YouTube video ID, primary key) - title, channelName, thumbnailUrl, durationSeconds - addedAt (timestamp), sourceType ('manual' | 'playlist') - playlistId (nullable FK) - sortOrder (integer for drag-and-drop)
Table: playlists - id (YouTube playlist ID, primary key) - title, channelName, lastSyncedAt - autoSync (boolean), syncIntervalMinutes
Table: studySessions - id (uuid, primary key) - videoId (FK → videos) - startTime, endTime (ISO timestamps) - focusedDurationSeconds (net focused time only) - playbackRate
Table: sessionEvents (granular event log) - id, sessionId (FK), eventType ('play'|'pause'|'focus_lost'|'focus_gained'|'rate_change') - timestamp, playbackRate
Include Dexie version declaration, index definitions, and migration hooks. All types must be strictly typed — no `any`. Place the entire schema + repository helper functions in /src/db/db.ts.

Detail the complete integration layer. Include:
URL Parsing: - Regex or URL API patterns for all YouTube URL formats: youtu.be/ID, watch?v=ID, playlist?list=ID, embed/ID, shorts/ID, /live/ID
Vercel Serverless Functions (/api/*): - /api/youtube/video.ts → videos.list (snippet + contentDetails) - /api/youtube/playlist.ts → playlistItems.list, paginated with nextPageToken - /api/youtube/sync.ts → lightweight sync check using etag or publishedAt - API key read from process.env.YOUTUBE_API_KEY (set in Vercel dashboard) - Each function validates input, calls Google API, returns only needed fields
Client (/src/services/youtube.ts): - Calls /api/youtube/* — never googleapis.com - Duration parsing: ISO 8601 PT#H#M#S → seconds - Caches responses in Dexie to minimize API calls
Auto-Sync: - setInterval + Page Visibility API (pause sync when tab is hidden) - Compare etag or latest publishedAt to detect new items
Error Handling: - 403 quota exceeded → user-facing message + exponential backoff - Invalid URL → client-side validation before any API call - Network failure → serve cached metadata from Dexie

Write the precise state machine logic (not pseudocode — actual TypeScript logic flow) for the session tracker hook: useSessionTracker(videoId).
The tracker must:
ACTIVE conditions (ALL must be true simultaneously): □ document.visibilityState === 'visible' □ document.hasFocus() === true □ YouTube player state === YT.PlayerState.PLAYING □ Not buffering (YT.PlayerState.BUFFERING === false)
PAUSE tracking when ANY condition fails. RESUME tracking automatically when all conditions restore.
Playback rate adjustment: focusedSeconds += wallClockElapsed * playbackRate
On session end (unmount or video change): - Write final StudySession record to Dexie - Append all sessionEvents log entries
Provide the complete hook implementation including: - useEffect dependency management - Interval-based accumulator (100ms tick) - Cleanup on unmount - YouTube Player event bindings (onStateChange, onPlaybackRateChange) - Page Visibility API listener - Window focus/blur listeners

Define the complete PWA service worker strategy:
Cache Strategy: - App shell (HTML, CSS, JS bundles) → Cache First - Static assets (fonts, icons) → Cache First, long TTL - /api/* responses → Network First, fallback to Dexie-cached data - YouTube thumbnails → Stale While Revalidate - Video streams → Network Only (never cache)
Implementation: - Use vite-plugin-pwa (Workbox under the hood) - Precache manifest generated at build time - Runtime caching rules per route pattern
PWA Manifest (in /public/manifest.json): - name, short_name, display: standalone - Icons: 192×192, 512×512 maskable - background_color: #ffffff, theme_color: #000000 - start_url: /
Vercel SPA Routing: - vercel.json must include a rewrite rule: { "source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html" } so deep links work correctly after PWA install
Install Prompt: - Intercept beforeinstallprompt - Defer and surface in UI as dismissable banner using lucide-react Download icon - Track install state in localStorage
Lighthouse PWA Checklist: - HTTPS enforced (automatic on Vercel) - start_url responds offline - Icons provided - Manifest linked in

Specify the drag-and-drop reordering strategy for the video library list.
Requirements: - Library: @dnd-kit/core + @dnd-kit/sortable (not react-beautiful-dnd) - Keyboard accessible (arrow keys to reorder) - Persist new sortOrder to Dexie on drop - Optimistic UI update before Dexie write - Handle touch events for mobile - Use lucide-react GripVertical icon as the drag handle
Provide: - SortableVideoList component structure - useSortable hook integration - onDragEnd handler with Dexie persistence logic - Accessibility aria-live announcements on reorder

Implement a GitHub-style study heatmap with zero external charting libs.
Data shape: { date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', totalMinutes: number }[]
Rendering: - Pure CSS Grid: 53 columns (weeks) × 7 rows (days) - Color scale: 4 intensity levels based on study duration thresholds (0, 1–30, 31–90, 91–180, 180+ minutes) - Strict white/black palette with opacity variants - Tooltip on hover: date + total study time - Month labels above grid
Data computation: - Query Dexie studySessions grouped by date - Aggregate focusedDurationSeconds → totalMinutes per day - Memoize with useMemo, recompute only when Dexie data changes
Provide the complete React component with inline Chakra UI styling. Keep it in a single file under /src/features/analytics/.

Implement the CSV export utility at /src/utils/exportCsv.ts.
Export schema (column order): date, videoId, videoTitle, channelName, startTime, endTime, focusedMinutes, playbackRate
Requirements: - Query all studySessions from Dexie, join videoId → videos table - Sort by startTime ascending - Escape commas and quotes per RFC 4180 - Generate Blob → createObjectURL → auto-trigger download - Filename: focustube-sessions-YYYY-MM-DD.csv - No external CSV library — implement the serializer natively - Provide TypeScript function signature: exportSessionsToCSV(): Promise

Provide the exact, copy-pasteable terminal sequence to scaffold the project from zero including: - pnpm create vite with React + TypeScript template - vercel.json for SPA fallback routing - All dependency installs (production + dev) in a single grouped block, including: chakra-ui, dexie, react-router-dom, @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, lucide-react, vite-plugin-pwa, uuid - tsconfig.json strict mode additions - ESLint config for strict React + TypeScript rules - Environment variable setup (.env.local template with VITE_ prefix note) - vite.config.ts with vite-plugin-pwa configuration No explanatory prose — commands only, with inline comments.

Recommend a deployment strategy optimized for Vercel hosting a Vite SPA: - vercel.json: SPA rewrite rules + serverless function configuration - Environment variables: YOUTUBE_API_KEY set per environment in Vercel dashboard (Production / Preview / Development separation) - Vercel Preview Deployments for PR-level testing - CDN: Vercel Edge Network serves /dist static assets automatically - YouTube API key rotation: update in Vercel dashboard → redeploy (no code change) - Rate limit monitoring via Vercel Analytics + Function logs - Custom domain + automatic HTTPS via Vercel (required for PWA service worker) Be specific — name actual Vercel features, not generic cloud concepts.

Address the YouTube Data API key exposure problem definitively.
Required solution: - API key lives in Vercel environment variables (server-only, never prefixed with VITE_) - All YouTube API calls proxied through Vercel Serverless Functions (/api/youtube/*) - Client code calls /api/youtube/* — googleapis.com never appears in the browser network tab - Each /api function: □ Validates YouTube ID/URL format before forwarding to Google □ Returns only the fields the client needs (no raw passthrough) □ Basic per-IP rate limiting via in-memory Map or Vercel KV if budget allows - Content Security Policy headers set in vercel.json headers config - .env.local is gitignored; .env.example committed with placeholder values
Provide the Vercel Serverless Function handler pattern (/api/youtube/video.ts) with input validation, API call, field filtering, and error handling applied. Use standard Web APIs (Request/Response) compatible with Vercel's Edge or Node.js runtime.

output_format:
Respond to each deliverable in sequence using this structure:
## [N]. [Title] [Content — code, schema, algorithm, or commands as appropriate]
Rules: - All TypeScript must be complete and compilable — no placeholder comments like `// implement this` - Inline code comments only where non-obvious - No motivational framing ("This is a great approach because...") - No restating of requirements before answering them - Where implementation choices exist, make the decision and justify it in one sentence maximum - Prefer simple, readable code over clever abstractions - Treat this as a real production codebase handoff
